problem: \[\text{Definition: } k \in \mathbf{N},\ \alpha, \beta \in \mathbf{R}.\]\[\text{Hypothesis: } \alpha \leq \beta.\]\[\text{Definition: for } a=(a_1,\dots,a_k) \in \mathbf{R}^k,\ p(t)=a_1+a_2 t+\cdots+a_k t^{k-1}\ \text{for } t \in \mathbf{R}.\]\[\text{Definition: } S=\{a \in \mathbf{R}^k \mid p(0)=1,\ |p(t)| \leq 1\ \text{for all } t \in [\alpha,\beta]\}.\]\[\text{Goal: determine whether } S \text{ is convex.}\]

\[\text{Definition: } C \subseteq \mathbf{R}^n.\]
\[\text{Definition: for a scalar } \alpha,\ \alpha C = \{\alpha x \mid x \in C\}.\]
\[\text{Definition: for scalars } \alpha, \beta,\ \alpha C + \beta C = \{u+v \mid u \in \alpha C,\ v \in \beta C\}.\]
\[\text{Goal: prove that } C \text{ is convex if and only if } (\alpha + \beta)C = \alpha C + \beta C \text{ for all } \alpha, \beta \ge 0.\]

\[\text{Definition: } A \in \mathbf{R}^{m\times n},\ b \in \mathbf{R}^m,\ c \in \mathbf{R}^n,\ d \in \mathbf{R}.\]\[\text{Definition: } E \in \mathbf{R}^{p\times m},\ f \in \mathbf{R}^p,\ g \in \mathbf{R}^m,\ h \in \mathbf{R}.\]\[\text{Definition: } \phi : \mathbf{R}^n \to \mathbf{R}^m,\ \phi(x) = \frac{Ax+b}{c^T x + d}.\]\[\text{Definition: } \psi : \mathbf{R}^m \to \mathbf{R}^p,\ \psi(y) = \frac{Ey+f}{g^T y + h}.\]\[\text{Definition: } \mathbf{dom}\,\phi = \{x \in \mathbf{R}^n \mid c^T x + d > 0\}.\]\[\text{Definition: } \mathbf{dom}\,\psi = \{y \in \mathbf{R}^m \mid g^T y + h > 0\}.\]\[\text{Definition: } M_\phi = \begin{bmatrix} A & b \\ c^T & d \end{bmatrix},\quad M_\psi = \begin{bmatrix} E & f \\ g^T & h \end{bmatrix}.\]\[\text{Definition: } \Gamma = \psi \circ \phi,\ \Gamma(x) = \psi(\phi(x)).\]\[\text{Definition: } \mathbf{dom}\,\Gamma = \{x \in \mathbf{dom}\,\phi \mid \phi(x) \in \mathbf{dom}\,\psi\}.\]\[\text{Goal: } \text{prove that } \Gamma \text{ is linear-fractional, and its associated matrix is } M_\psi M_\phi = \begin{bmatrix} E & f \\ g^T & h \end{bmatrix}\begin{bmatrix} A & b \\ c^T & d \end{bmatrix}.\]

\[\text{Definition: } K_{\exp} = \{(x,y,z) \in \mathbf{R}^3 \mid y > 0,\ ye^{x/y} \leq z\}.\]\[\text{Definition: } K_{\exp}^* = \{u \in \mathbf{R}^3 \mid u^T v \geq 0\ \text{for all } v \in K_{\exp}\}.\]\[\text{Hypothesis: boundary issues may be ignored.}\]\[\text{Hypothesis: } K_{\exp} \text{ is not closed, and its closure is obtained by adding } \{(x,y,z) \mid x \leq 0,\ y = 0,\ z \geq 0\}.\]\[\text{Hypothesis: the dual of a cone is equal to the dual of its closure.}\]\[\text{Goal: determine } K_{\exp}^*.\]

Definition: $C, D \subseteq \mathbf{R}^n$.\\
Hypothesis: $C$ and $D$ are closed convex cones.\\
Definition: for any cone $K \subseteq \mathbf{R}^n$, its dual cone is $K^* = \{y \in \mathbf{R}^n \mid y^T x \ge 0 \text{ for all } x \in K\}$.\\
Definition: $C^* + D^* = \{u + v \mid u \in C^*,\ v \in D^*\}$.\\
Goal: prove that $C \cap D$ is a convex cone.\\
Goal: prove that $C^* + D^*$ is a convex cone.

\[\begin{array}{l}
\text{Definition: } C, D \subseteq \mathbf{R}^n \text{ are closed convex cones.}\\
\text{Definition: For any cone } K \subseteq \mathbf{R}^n,\ K^* = \{y \in \mathbf{R}^n \mid y^T x \ge 0 \text{ for all } x \in K\}.\\
\text{Definition: } C^* + D^* = \{u + v \mid u \in C^*,\ v \in D^*\}.\\
\text{Goal: prove that } (C \cap D)^* \supseteq C^* + D^*.
\end{array}\]

Definition: $C, D$ are closed convex cones in $\mathbf{R}^n$.\\ Definition: For any cone $K \subseteq \mathbf{R}^n$, define $K^* = \{y \in \mathbf{R}^n \mid y^T x \ge 0 \text{ for all } x \in K\}$.\\ Definition: $C^* + D^* = \{u+v \mid u \in C^*,\ v \in D^*\}$.\\ Hypothesis: For every closed convex cone $K \subseteq \mathbf{R}^n$, one has $K^{**} = K$.\\ Goal: prove $(C \cap D)^* \subseteq C^* + D^*$.\\ Goal: first prove that $(C \cap D)^* \subseteq C^* + D^*$ if and only if $C \cap D \supseteq (C^* + D^*)^*$.\\ Goal: then prove $C \cap D \supseteq (C^* + D^*)^*$.\\ Goal: conclude that $(C \cap D)^* = C^* + D^*$.

\[\text{Definition: } A \in \mathbf{R}^{m \times n}.\]
\[\text{Definition: } V = \{x \in \mathbf{R}^n \mid Ax \succeq 0\}.\]
\[\text{Definition: } \{A^T v \mid v \succeq 0\} = \{A^T v \mid v \in \mathbf{R}^m,\ v \succeq 0\}.\]
\[\text{Hypothesis: } Ax \succeq 0 \text{ means that each component of } Ax \text{ is nonnegative.}\]
\[\text{Hypothesis: } v \succeq 0 \text{ means that each component of } v \text{ is nonnegative.}\]
\[\text{Goal: prove that } V^* = \{A^T v \mid v \succeq 0\}.\]

\[\text{Definition: } C \subseteq \mathbf{R}^n.\]
\[\text{Definition: } C^\circ = \{y \in \mathbf{R}^n \mid y^T x \leq 1 \text{ for all } x \in C\}.\]
\[\text{Goal: prove that } C^\circ \text{ is convex.}\]

\[\text{Definition: } C \subseteq \mathbf{R}^n.\]
\[\text{Definition: } C^\circ = \{y \in \mathbf{R}^n \mid y^T x \leq 1 \text{ for all } x \in C\}.\]
\[\text{Hypothesis: } C \text{ is a cone, i.e., for all } x \in C \text{ and all } \theta \ge 0,\ \theta x \in C.\]
\[\text{Goal: determine } C^\circ.\]

\[\text{Definition: } C \subseteq \mathbf{R}^n,\quad C^\circ = \{y \in \mathbf{R}^n \mid y^T x \leq 1 \text{ for all } x \in C\}.\]\[\text{Definition: } \|\cdot\| \text{ is a norm on } \mathbf{R}^n.\]\[\text{Definition: } B = \{x \in \mathbf{R}^n \mid \|x\| \leq 1\}.\]\[\text{Goal: determine } B^\circ.\]

\[\text{Definition: } \mathbf{1} \in \mathbf{R}^n \text{ is the all-ones vector.}\]
\[\text{Definition: } C = \{x \in \mathbf{R}^n \mid \mathbf{1}^T x = 1,\ x \succeq 0\}.\]
\[\text{Definition: } C^\circ = \{y \in \mathbf{R}^n \mid y^T x \le 1 \text{ for all } x \in C\}.\]
\[\text{Goal: determine } C^\circ.\]

Definition: $C \subseteq \mathbf{R}^n$. \\ Definition: $C^\circ = \{y \in \mathbf{R}^n \mid y^T x \le 1 \text{ for all } x \in C\}$. \\ Hypothesis: $C$ is closed. \\ Hypothesis: $C$ is convex. \\ Hypothesis: $0 \in C$. \\ Goal: prove that $(C^\circ)^\circ = C$.

\[\text{Definition: } K \subseteq \mathbf{R}^2,\quad K = \{0\}.\]
\[\text{Definition: } K^* = \{y \in \mathbf{R}^2 \mid y^T x \ge 0 \text{ for all } x \in K\}.\]
\[\text{Goal: describe the dual cone } K^*.\]

Definition: $K \subseteq \mathbf{R}^2$.\\
Definition: $K^* = \{y \in \mathbf{R}^2 \mid y^T x \ge 0 \text{ for all } x \in K\}$.\\
Hypothesis: $K = \mathbf{R}^2$.\\
Goal: describe the dual cone $K^*$.

\[\text{Definition: } K = \{(x_1, x_2) \in \mathbf{R}^2 \mid |x_1| \leq x_2\}.\]
\[\text{Definition: } K^* = \{y \in \mathbf{R}^2 \mid y^T x \ge 0 \text{ for all } x \in K\}.\]
\[\text{Goal: describe } K^*.\]

\[\text{Definition: } K = \{(x_1, x_2) \in \mathbf{R}^2 \mid x_1 + x_2 = 0\}.\]
\[\text{Definition: } K^* = \{y \in \mathbf{R}^2 \mid y^T x \ge 0 \text{ for all } x \in K\}.\]
\[\text{Goal: describe the dual cone } K^*.\]

\[\text{Definition: } v_1,\ldots,v_k \in \mathbb{R}^n.\]
\[\text{Definition: } P = \operatorname{conv}\{v_1,\ldots,v_k\} \subseteq \mathbb{R}^n.\]
\[\text{Definition: } f : \mathbb{R}^n \to \mathbb{R}.\]
\[\text{Hypothesis: } f \text{ is convex on } \mathbb{R}^n.\]
\[\text{Goal: } \sup_{x \in P} f(x) = \max_{i=1,\ldots,k} f(v_i).\]

\[\text{Definition: } h : \mathbf{R}^k \to \mathbf{R}.\]\[\text{Hypothesis: } h \text{ is convex and } \operatorname{dom} h = \mathbf{R}^k.\]\[\text{Definition: For each } i=1,\dots,k,\ g_i : \mathbf{R}^n \to \mathbf{R}.\]\[\text{Definition: } f : \mathbf{R}^n \to \mathbf{R}, \quad f(x) = h(g_1(x), g_2(x), \ldots, g_k(x)).\]\[\text{Hypothesis: For each } i \in \{1,\dots,k\}, \text{ at least one of the following holds.}\]\[\text{Hypothesis: } h \text{ is nondecreasing in the } i\text{th argument, and } g_i \text{ is convex.}\]\[\text{Hypothesis: } h \text{ is nonincreasing in the } i\text{th argument, and } g_i \text{ is concave.}\]\[\text{Hypothesis: } g_i \text{ is affine.}\]\[\text{Goal: Prove that } f \text{ is convex on } \mathbf{R}^n.\]

Definition: $u \in \mathbf{R}^n$ and $t \in \mathbf{R}_{++}$.

Definition: $g(u,t) = t - (1/t) u^T u$.

Definition: $g$ is defined on $\{(u,t) \in \mathbf{R}^n \times \mathbf{R}_{++}\}$.

Definition: $\operatorname{dom} f = \{(u,t) \in \mathbf{R}^n \times \mathbf{R} \mid t > \|u\|_2\}$.

Goal: explain why $g$ is concave on $\operatorname{dom} f$.

Hypothesis: use the convexity of the quadratic-over-linear function.

\[\text{Definition: } u \in \mathbf{R}^n \text{ and } t \in \mathbf{R}.\]
\[\text{Definition: } g(u,t) = -\log\!\left(t - \frac{1}{t} u^T u\right).\]
\[\text{Definition: } \operatorname{dom} f = \{(u,t) \in \mathbf{R}^n \times \mathbf{R} \mid t > \|u\|_2\}.\]
\[\text{Hypothesis: use the result established previously.}\]
\[\text{Goal: prove that } g \text{ is convex on } \operatorname{dom} f.\]

\[\text{Definition: } f(x,t) = -\log(t^2 - x^T x), \quad (x,t) \in \mathbf{R}^n \times \mathbf{R}.\]
\[\text{Definition: } \operatorname{dom} f = \{(x,t) \in \mathbf{R}^n \times \mathbf{R} \mid t > \|x\|_2\}.\]
\[\text{Hypothesis: } f(x,t) = -\log(t - \|x\|_2) - \log(t + \|x\|_2).\]
\[\text{Hypothesis: this representation follows from standard convexity-preserving composition rules, using convexity of } -\log(\cdot) \text{ and properties of } t \pm \|x\|_2 \text{ on } t > \|x\|_2.\]
\[\text{Goal: prove that } f \text{ is convex on } \operatorname{dom} f.\]

\[\begin{aligned} &\text{Definition: } f : \mathbf{R}^n \to \mathbf{R}. \\ &\text{Definition: } g : \mathbf{R}^n \to \mathbf{R}. \\ &\text{Definition: } h(x) = \frac{f(x)^2}{g(x)}. \\ &\text{Definition: } \operatorname{dom} h = \operatorname{dom} f \cap \operatorname{dom} g. \\ &\text{Hypothesis: } f \text{ is convex.} \\ &\text{Hypothesis: } f(x) \ge 0 \text{ for all } x \in \operatorname{dom} f. \\ &\text{Hypothesis: } g \text{ is concave.} \\ &\text{Hypothesis: } g(x) > 0 \text{ for all } x \in \operatorname{dom} g. \\ &\text{Goal: prove that } h \text{ is convex on } \operatorname{dom} h.\end{aligned}\]

\[\begin{array}{l}
\text{Definition: } f : \mathbf{R}^n \to \mathbf{R}.\\
\text{Hypothesis: } f \text{ is convex.}\\
\text{Hypothesis: } \operatorname{dom} f = \mathbf{R}^n.\\
\text{Hypothesis: } f(0) \leq 0.\\
\text{Definition: } g(x,t) = t f(x/t).\\
\text{Definition: } g \text{ is defined for } (x,t) \text{ such that } t > 0.\\
\text{Definition: } x \in \mathbf{R}^n \text{ is fixed.}\\
\text{Goal: prove that the function } t \mapsto t f(x/t) \text{ is nonincreasing on } t > 0.
\end{array}\]

\[\text{Definition: } f : \mathbf{R}^n \to \mathbf{R}.\]\[\text{Definition: } g : \mathbf{R}^n \to \mathbf{R}.\]\[\text{Hypothesis: } f \text{ is convex and } f(0) \le 0.\]\[\text{Hypothesis: } g \text{ is concave.}\]\[\text{Hypothesis: } g(x) > 0 \text{ for all } x \in \operatorname{dom} g.\]\[\text{Definition: } h(x) = g(x) f\bigl(x / g(x)\bigr).\]\[\text{Definition: } \operatorname{dom} h = \{x \in \operatorname{dom} g \mid x / g(x) \in \operatorname{dom} f\}.\]\[\text{Goal: prove that } h \text{ is convex on } \operatorname{dom} h.\]

Definition: $\mathbf{R}_{++}^n = \{x \in \mathbf{R}^n \mid x_k > 0,\ k = 1, \dots, n\}$.\\
Definition: $h : \mathbf{R}_{++}^n \to \mathbf{R}$ is defined by $h(x) = \dfrac{x^T x}{\left(\prod_{k=1}^n x_k\right)^{1/n}}$.\\
Hypothesis: $\operatorname{dom} h = \mathbf{R}_{++}^n$.\\
Goal: prove that $h$ is convex on $\mathbf{R}_{++}^n$.

\[\begin{aligned}
&\text{Definition: } X \in \mathbf{S}_{++}^n,\ t \in \mathbf{R}_{++}.\\
&\text{Definition: } f(X,t)=nt\log t-t\log\det X.\\
&\text{Definition: } \operatorname{dom} f=\mathbf{S}_{++}^n\times \mathbf{R}_{++}.\\
&\text{Goal: prove that } f \text{ is convex in } (X,t).\\
&\text{Definition: } g:\mathbf{S}_{++}^n\to \mathbf{R}.\\
&\text{Definition: } g(X)=n(\operatorname{tr}X)\log(\operatorname{tr}X)-(\operatorname{tr}X)(\log\det X).\\
&\text{Definition: if } \lambda_1,\dots,\lambda_n \text{ are the eigenvalues of } X, \text{ then }\\
&\qquad g(X)=n\left(\sum_{i=1}^n \lambda_i\right)\left(\log\sum_{i=1}^n \lambda_i-\sum_{i=1}^n \log\lambda_i\right).\\
&\text{Goal: using the convexity of } f, \text{ prove that } g \text{ is convex on } \mathbf{S}_{++}^n.
\end{aligned}\]

\[	ext{Definition: } f : \mathbf{R}^m 	o \mathbf{R}.\]
\[	ext{Definition: } A \in \mathbf{R}^{m 	imes n},\ b \in \mathbf{R}^m,\ c \in \mathbf{R}^n,\ d \in \mathbf{R}.\]
\[	ext{Hypothesis: } f 	ext{ is convex.}\]
\[	ext{Definition: } \operatorname{dom} g = \{x \in \mathbf{R}^n \mid c^T x + d > 0\}.\]
\[	ext{Definition: for } x \in \operatorname{dom} g,\ g(x) = (c^T x + d)\, f\!\left( rac{Ax+b}{c^T x+d}ight).\]
\[	ext{Definition: }  rac{Ax+b}{c^T x+d} 	ext{ denotes division of the vector } Ax+b \in \mathbf{R}^m 	ext{ by the positive scalar } c^T x+d.\]
\[	ext{Goal: prove that } g 	ext{ is convex on } \operatorname{dom} g.\]

\[\text{Definition: } a,c \in \mathbf{R}^n \text{ and } b,d \in \mathbf{R}.\]
\[\text{Definition: } f : \mathbf{R}^n \to \mathbf{R}, \quad f(x)=\frac{a^T x+b}{c^T x+d}.\]
\[\text{Definition: } \operatorname{dom} f = \{x \in \mathbf{R}^n \mid c^T x + d > 0\}.\]
\[\text{Hypothesis: } \operatorname{dom} f \text{ is nonempty.}\]
\[\text{Goal: determine the conditions under which } f \text{ is convex.}\]
\[\text{Goal: determine the conditions under which } f \text{ is quasiconvex.}\]

\[\text{Definition: } A \in \mathbb{R}^{m \times n},\ b \in \mathbb{R}^m.\]\[\text{Definition: } f : \mathbb{R}^n \to \mathbb{R},\quad f(x) = \frac{\|Ax-b\|_2^2}{1-x^T x}.\]\[\text{Definition: } B = \{x \in \mathbb{R}^n \mid \|x\|_2 < 1\}.\]\[\text{Goal: prove that } f \text{ is convex on } B.\]

\[\text{Definition: } n \in \mathbf{N}.\]
\[\text{Definition: } \alpha_1,\dots,\alpha_n \in \mathbf{R}.\]
\[\text{Hypothesis: } \alpha_k \ge 0 \text{ for } k=1,\dots,n.\]
\[\text{Hypothesis: } \sum_{k=1}^n \alpha_k \le 1.\]
\[\text{Definition: } f : \mathbf{R}_{++}^n \to \mathbf{R}, \quad f(x)=\prod_{k=1}^n x_k^{\alpha_k}.\]
\[\text{Goal: prove that } f \text{ is concave on } \mathbf{R}_{++}^n.\]

Definition: $f : \mathbf{R}^n \to \mathbf{R}$.\\
Hypothesis: $f$ is convex.\\
Definition: $g : \operatorname{dom} g \to \mathbf{R}$ is defined by $g(x,t) = f(x/t)$.\\
Definition: $\operatorname{dom} g = \{(x,t) \in \mathbf{R}^n \times \mathbf{R} \mid x/t \in \operatorname{dom} f,\ t > 0\}$.\\
Goal: prove that $g$ is quasiconvex.

Definition: $x = (x_1,x_2,x_3,x_4) \in \mathbf{R}^4$.\\
Definition: $f : D \to \mathbf{R}$ is defined by
\[
f(x)=\frac{1}{x_1-\frac{1}{x_2-\frac{1}{x_3-\frac{1}{x_4}}}}.
\]\\
Definition: $D$ is the set of all $x \in \mathbf{R}^4$ such that every denominator in the continued fraction is positive.\\
Hypothesis: $x_4 > 0$.\\
Hypothesis: $x_3-\frac{1}{x_4} > 0$.\\
Hypothesis: $x_2-\frac{1}{x_3-\frac{1}{x_4}} > 0$.\\
Hypothesis: $x_1-\frac{1}{x_2-\frac{1}{x_3-\frac{1}{x_4}}} > 0$.\\
Goal: prove that $f$ is convex on $D$.\\
Goal: prove that $f$ is decreasing in each variable.

Definition: $n \in \mathbf{N}$ and $x \in \mathbf{R}^n$.\\ Definition: The scalar Huber function is $f_{\mathrm{hub}} : \mathbf{R} \to \mathbf{R}$, given by \[ f_{\mathrm{hub}}(t) = \begin{cases} (1/2)t^2 & |t| \leq 1 \\ |t| - 1/2 & |t| > 1. \end{cases} \]\\ Definition: The circularly symmetric Huber function is $f_{\mathrm{cshub}} : \mathbf{R}^n \to \mathbf{R}$, defined by $f_{\mathrm{cshub}}(x) = f_{\mathrm{hub}}(\|x\|_2)$. Equivalently, \[ f_{\mathrm{cshub}}(x) = \begin{cases} (1/2)\|x\|_2^2 & \|x\|_2 \leq 1 \\ \|x\|_2 - 1/2 & \|x\|_2 > 1. \end{cases} \]\\ Hypothesis: $\|\cdot\|_2$ is the Euclidean norm.\\ Goal: prove that $f_{\mathrm{cshub}}$ is convex.\\ Goal: determine the conjugate function $f_{\mathrm{cshub}}^*$.

\[\text{Definition: } f : \mathbf{R}^m \to \mathbf{R} \cup \{+\infty\}.\]
\[\text{Definition: } x_1, \ldots, x_n \in \operatorname{dom} f.\]
\[\text{Definition: } \lambda_1, \ldots, \lambda_n \text{ are scalars}.\]
\[\text{Hypothesis: } f \text{ is convex}.\]
\[\text{Hypothesis: } \lambda_1 > 0.\]
\[\text{Hypothesis: } \lambda_i \leq 0 \text{ for } i = 2, \ldots, n.\]
\[\text{Hypothesis: } \lambda_1 + \cdots + \lambda_n = 1.\]
\[\text{Goal: prove that } f(\lambda_1 x_1 + \cdots + \lambda_n x_n) \geq \lambda_1 f(x_1) + \cdots + \lambda_n f(x_n).\]

Definition: $f : \mathbf{R}^n \to \mathbf{R}$.\\
Hypothesis: $f$ is convex.\\
Definition: A function $h : \mathbf{R}^n \to \mathbf{R}$ is monotone nondecreasing if, for all $x,y \in \mathbf{R}^n$, $x \ge y$ componentwise implies $h(x) \ge h(y)$.\\
Definition: For $x \in \mathbf{R}^n$, define
$$g(x) = \inf_{z \in \mathbf{R}^n,\ z \ge 0} f(x+z),$$
where $z \ge 0$ is understood componentwise.\\
Hypothesis: For all $x \in \mathbf{R}^n$, $g(x) > -\infty$.\\
Goal: prove that $g$ is convex.\\
Goal: prove that $g$ is monotone nondecreasing.\\
Goal: prove that $g(x) \le f(x)$ for all $x \in \mathbf{R}^n$.\\
Goal: if $h : \mathbf{R}^n \to \mathbf{R}$ is convex, monotone nondecreasing, and satisfies $h(x) \le f(x)$ for all $x \in \mathbf{R}^n$, then prove that $h(x) \le g(x)$ for all $x \in \mathbf{R}^n$.\\
Goal: conclude that $g$ is the maximum convex monotone underestimator of $f$.

\[\text{Definition: } f : \mathbf{R}^n \to \mathbf{R}.\]\[\text{Hypothesis: } f \text{ is convex.}\]\[\text{Hypothesis: for every orthogonal matrix } Q \in \mathbf{R}^{n \times n} \text{ and every } x \in \mathbf{R}^n,\ f(Qx) = f(x).\]\[\text{Equivalent description: } f(x) \text{ depends only on } \|x\|_2.\]\[\text{Goal: prove that there exists } \phi : \mathbf{R} \to \mathbf{R} \text{ such that } \operatorname{dom} \phi = \mathbf{R},\ \phi \text{ is convex, } \phi \text{ is nondecreasing, and } f(x) = \phi(\|x\|_2) \text{ for all } x \in \mathbf{R}^n.\]

\[\text{Definition: } f_1,\ldots,f_m : \mathbf{R}^n \to \mathbf{R} \cup \{+\infty\}.\]\[\text{Hypothesis: each } f_i \text{ is convex.}\]\[\text{Definition: } g = f_1 \diamond \cdots \diamond f_m.\]\[\text{Definition: for } x \in \mathbf{R}^n,\quad g(x)=\inf\{f_1(x_1)+\cdots+f_m(x_m) \mid x_1,\ldots,x_m \in \mathbf{R}^n,\ x_1+\cdots+x_m=x\}.\]\[\text{Definition: } \operatorname{dom} g = \{x \in \mathbf{R}^n \mid g(x) < +\infty\}.\]\[\text{Goal: prove that } g \text{ is convex.}\]

Definition: $f_1, \ldots, f_m : \mathbf{R}^n \to \mathbf{R} \cup \{\infty\}$.\\
Hypothesis: Each $f_i$ is convex.\\
Definition: $g = f_1 \diamond \cdots \diamond f_m$.\\
Definition: For each $x \in \mathbf{R}^n$, $$g(x) = \inf\{f_1(x_1) + \cdots + f_m(x_m) \mid x_1, \ldots, x_m \in \mathbf{R}^n,\ x_1 + \cdots + x_m = x\}.$$\\
Definition: $\operatorname{dom} g = \{x \in \mathbf{R}^n \mid g(x) < \infty\}$.\\
Definition: For each $i$, the convex conjugate of $f_i$ is $$f_i^*(y) = \sup_{x \in \mathbf{R}^n} (y^T x - f_i(x)).$$\\
Definition: The convex conjugate of $g$ is $$g^*(y) = \sup_{x \in \mathbf{R}^n} (y^T x - g(x)).$$\\
Goal: prove that $$g^* = f_1^* + \cdots + f_m^*.$$

\[\text{Definition: } A \in \mathbf{R}^{m \times n},\ f : \mathbf{R}^m \to \mathbf{R},\ g : \mathbf{R}^n \to \mathbf{R}.\]\[\text{Hypothesis: } \operatorname{rank} A = m.\]\[\text{Hypothesis: } f \text{ is convex}.\]\[\text{Definition: } g(x) = f(Ax) \text{ for all } x \in \mathbf{R}^n.\]\[\text{Definition: } f^*(u) = \sup_{z \in \mathbf{R}^m} \bigl(u^T z - f(z)\bigr) \text{ for } u \in \mathbf{R}^m.\]\[\text{Definition: } g^*(y) = \sup_{x \in \mathbf{R}^n} \bigl(y^T x - g(x)\bigr) \text{ for } y \in \mathbf{R}^n.\]\[\text{Definition: } A^\dagger = (A A^T)^{-1} A.\]\[\text{Goal: for every } y \in \mathbf{R}^n,\ g^*(y) = f^*\bigl((A^\dagger)^T y\bigr).\]\[\text{Goal: } \operatorname{dom}(g^*) = A^T \operatorname{dom}(f^*).\]

Definition: $n \ge 1$.\\ Definition: $\lambda_1, \ldots, \lambda_n$ are positive real numbers.\\ Definition: $f : \mathbf{R}^n \to \mathbf{R}$ is defined by $f(x) = \prod_{i=1}^n (1 - e^{-x_i})^{\lambda_i}$.\\ Definition: $\operatorname{dom} f = \left\{ x \in \mathbf{R}_{++}^n \mid \sum_{i=1}^n \lambda_i e^{-x_i} \le 1 \right\}$.\\ Goal: prove that $f$ is concave on $\operatorname{dom} f$.

\[\text{Definition: } a,b \in \mathbf{R}.\]
\[\text{Hypothesis: } a < b.\]
\[\text{Definition: } x=(x_1,\dots,x_n) \in \mathbf{R}^n.\]
\[\text{Definition: } p_x(t)=x_1+x_2 t+x_3 t^2+\cdots+x_n t^{n-1}, \quad t \in [a,b].\]
\[\text{Definition: } f : \mathbf{R}^n \to \mathbf{R}, \quad f(x)=\sup_{t \in [a,b]} p_x(t)-\inf_{t \in [a,b]} p_x(t).\]
\[\text{Goal: prove that } f \text{ is convex on } \mathbf{R}^n.\]

Definition: $m \in \mathbf{N}$, and for each $i = 1, \ldots, m$, $f_i : \mathbf{R}^n \to \mathbf{R}$.\\
Hypothesis: For each $i = 1, \ldots, m$, the function $f_i$ is convex.\\
Definition: $\operatorname{dom} f = \{x \in \mathbf{R}^n \mid f_i(x) < 0,\ i = 1, \ldots, m\}$.\\
Definition: For $x \in \operatorname{dom} f$, $f(x) = \sum_{i=1}^m e^{-1/f_i(x)}$.\\
Goal: prove that $f$ is convex on $\operatorname{dom} f$.

\[\text{Definition: } g : \mathbf{R}^n \to \mathbf{R} \cup \{+\infty\}.\]
\[\text{Definition: } \operatorname{dom} g := \{ z \in \mathbf{R}^n \mid g(z) < +\infty \}.\]
\[\text{Definition: } y \in \operatorname{dom} g.\]
\[\text{Hypothesis: } g \text{ is convex.}\]
\[\text{Definition: } f(x) = \inf_{\alpha > 0} \frac{g(y+\alpha x)-g(y)}{\alpha}, \quad x \in \mathbf{R}^n.\]
\[\text{Hypothesis: the infimum is taken in the extended-real sense.}\]
\[\text{Goal: prove that } f \text{ is convex.}\]

Definition: $f : \mathbf{R}^n \to \mathbf{R}$.\\
Hypothesis: $f$ is convex.\\
Hypothesis: $f$ is symmetric, i.e., $f(x) = f(Px)$ for every permutation matrix $P \in \mathbf{R}^{n \times n}$.\\
Definition: $S \in \mathbf{R}^{n \times n}$.\\
Hypothesis: $S$ is doubly stochastic, i.e., its entries are nonnegative and every row sum and every column sum are equal to $1$.\\
Definition: $x \in \mathbf{R}^n$.\\
Goal: prove that $f(Sx) \leq f(x)$.

Definition: $Y \in S^n$. \\ Definition: $Y = Q \, \operatorname{diag}(\lambda) \, Q^T$, where $Q \in \mathbf{R}^{n \times n}$ is orthogonal and $\lambda = (\lambda_1, \ldots, \lambda_n) \in \mathbf{R}^n$ is the vector of eigenvalues of $Y$. \\ Definition: $S \in \mathbf{R}^{n \times n}$ is defined by $S_{ij} = Q_{ij}^2$ for all $i,j$. \\ Goal: prove that $S$ is doubly stochastic. \\ Goal: prove that $\operatorname{diag}(Y) = S\lambda$.

Definition: $f : \mathbf{R}^n \to \mathbf{R}$.\\
Hypothesis: $f$ is symmetric, i.e., $f(x) = f(Px)$ for every permutation matrix $P \in \mathbf{R}^{n \times n}$.\\
Hypothesis: $f$ is convex.\\
Definition: $S^n$ is the space of $n \times n$ real symmetric matrices.\\
Definition: for $X \in S^n$, let $\lambda(X) = (\lambda_1(X), \ldots, \lambda_n(X)) \in \mathbf{R}^n$ be the eigenvalue vector of $X$.\\
Definition: for any square matrix $A$, let $\operatorname{diag}(A) \in \mathbf{R}^n$ be the vector of diagonal entries of $A$.\\
Definition: $\mathcal{V}$ is the set of $n \times n$ real orthogonal matrices.\\
Definition: $X \in S^n$.\\
Hypothesis: the results from parts (a) and (b) are available.\\
Goal: prove that
$$f(\lambda(X)) = \sup_{V \in \mathcal{V}} f(\operatorname{diag}(V^T X V)).$$\\
Goal: deduce that $f(\lambda(X))$ is convex as a function of $X$.

Definition: $n \ge 1$ is an integer.\\
Definition: $f : \mathbf{R}^{n \times n} \times \mathbf{R}^n \to \mathbf{R}$ is given by $f(X,y) = y^T X^{-1} y$.\\
Definition: $\operatorname{dom} f = \{(X,y) \mid X + X^T \succ 0\}$, where $X \in \mathbf{R}^{n \times n}$ and $y \in \mathbf{R}^n$.\\
Definition: $S^n$ is the set of real symmetric $n \times n$ matrices.\\
Hypothesis: For $X \in S^n$, the restricted function is known to be convex.\\
Goal: determine whether $f$ is convex on $\operatorname{dom} f$. If it is convex, prove it. If it is not convex, give a simple counterexample.

Definition: $\operatorname{dom} g \subseteq \mathbf{R}^n$ is a convex domain.\\
Definition: $g$ is twice differentiable on $\operatorname{dom} g$.\\
Definition: $f(x) = -\exp(-g(x))$ for $x \in \operatorname{dom} g$.\\
Hypothesis: for every $x \in \operatorname{dom} g$,
$$
\begin{bmatrix}
\nabla^2 g(x) & \nabla g(x) \\
\nabla g(x)^T & 1
\end{bmatrix} \succeq 0.
$$\\
Goal: prove that $f$ is convex on $\operatorname{dom} g$.

\[\text{Definition: } A \in \mathbf{R}^{m \times n}.\]\[\text{Definition: } b \in \mathbf{R}^m.\]\[\text{Definition: } \|\cdot\| \text{ is a norm on } \mathbf{R}^m.\]\[\text{Definition: } f : \mathbf{R}^n \to \mathbf{R}, \quad f(x) = \max\{\|APx - b\| \mid P \in \mathbf{R}^{n \times n} \text{ is a permutation matrix}\}.\]\[\text{Goal: prove that } f \text{ is convex.}\]

\[\text{Definition: } g,h : \mathbf{R}^n \to \mathbf{R}.\]\[\text{Hypothesis: } g \text{ and } h \text{ are convex.}\]\[\text{Hypothesis: } g \text{ and } h \text{ are bounded below.}\]\[\text{Hypothesis: } \operatorname{dom} g = \operatorname{dom} h = \mathbf{R}^n.\]\[\text{Definition: } f(x) = \inf\{\theta g(y) + (1-\theta) h(z) \mid y,z \in \mathbf{R}^n,\ \theta y + (1-\theta) z = x,\ 0 \leq \theta \leq 1\}.\]\[\text{Goal: prove that } f \text{ is convex.}\]\[\text{Goal: describe } \operatorname{epi} f \text{ in terms of } \operatorname{epi} g \text{ and } \operatorname{epi} h.\]

\[\text{Definition: } f : \mathbf{R} \to \mathbf{R} \cup \{+\infty\}.\]
\[\text{Definition: } \operatorname{dom} f = \{x \in \mathbf{R} \mid f(x) < +\infty\}.\]
\[\text{Goal: prove that } f \text{ is convex if and only if } \operatorname{dom} f \text{ is convex and the following holds.}\]
\[\text{Hypothesis: for all } x,y,z \in \operatorname{dom} f \text{ with } x<y<z,\]
\[\det\begin{bmatrix}1 & 1 & 1 \\ x & y & z \\ f(x) & f(y) & f(z)\end{bmatrix} \geq 0.\]

\[\text{Definition: } Y, Z \in \mathbf{S}^m.\]
\[\text{Hypothesis: } 0 \prec Y \preceq Z.\]
\[\text{Goal: prove that } \det Y \leq \det Z.\]

Definition: $X \in \mathbf{S}_{++}^n$.\\
Definition: Partition $X$ as
\[
X=
\begin{bmatrix}
X_{11} & X_{12} \\
X_{12}^T & X_{22}
\end{bmatrix},
\]
with $X_{11} \in \mathbf{S}^m$, $X_{12} \in \mathbf{R}^{m\times (n-m)}$, and $X_{22} \in \mathbf{S}^{n-m}$.\\
Definition: Consider the optimization problem with variable $Y \in \mathbf{S}^m$:
\[
\begin{aligned}
\text{minimize}\quad & \log\det Y^{-1} \\
\text{subject to}\quad & \begin{bmatrix}
Y & 0 \\
0 & 0
\end{bmatrix}
\preceq
\begin{bmatrix}
X_{11} & X_{12} \\
X_{12}^T & X_{22}
\end{bmatrix}.
\end{aligned}
\]
Hypothesis: The domain of $\log\det Y^{-1}$ is $\mathbf{S}_{++}^m$.\\
Goal: prove that the solution is
\[
Y = X_{11} - X_{12}X_{22}^{-1}X_{12}^T.
\]

Definition: $P = \begin{bmatrix} I \\ 0 \end{bmatrix} \in \mathbf{R}^{n \times m}$, where $I$ is the $m \times m$ identity matrix.\\
Definition: $X \in \mathbf{S}_{++}^n$ and $X$ is partitioned conformally with $P$ as $X = \begin{bmatrix} X_{11} & X_{12} \\ X_{12}^T & X_{22} \end{bmatrix}$.\\
Definition: $X_{11} \in \mathbf{S}^m$, $X_{22} \in \mathbf{S}^{n-m}$, and $X_{12} \in \mathbf{R}^{m \times (n-m)}$.\\
Definition: $f : \mathbf{S}^n \to \mathbf{R}$ is given by $f(X) = \log \det \left( X_{11} - X_{12} X_{22}^{-1} X_{12}^T \right)^{-1}$.\\
Hypothesis: $\operatorname{dom} f = \mathbf{S}_{++}^n$.\\
Hypothesis: use the result from part (b) and the minimization property on page 3-19 of the lecture notes.\\
Goal: prove that $f$ is convex.

\[\text{Definition: } P \in \mathbf{R}^{n \times m},\ \operatorname{rank}(P)=m,\ \text{and } P = \begin{bmatrix} I \\ 0 \end{bmatrix},\ \text{where } I \text{ is the } m \times m \text{ identity matrix}.\]
\[\text{Definition: } f : \mathbf{S}^n \to \mathbf{R},\ \operatorname{dom} f = \mathbf{S}_{++}^n,\ \text{and } f(X) = \log \det(P^T X^{-1} P).\]
\[\text{Definition: } X \in \mathbf{S}_{++}^n \text{ is partitioned conformably with } P \text{ as } X = \begin{bmatrix} X_{11} & X_{12} \\ X_{12}^T & X_{22} \end{bmatrix}.\]
\[\text{Definition: } X_{11} \in \mathbf{S}^m,\ X_{22} \in \mathbf{S}^{n-m},\ X_{12} \in \mathbf{R}^{m \times (n-m)}.\]
\[\text{Goal: prove that } (X_{11} - X_{12} X_{22}^{-1} X_{12}^T)^{-1} = P^T X^{-1} P.\]
\[\text{Goal: conclude that } (X_{11} - X_{12} X_{22}^{-1} X_{12}^T)^{-1} \text{ is the leading } m \times m \text{ principal submatrix of } X^{-1}.\]
\[\text{Goal: conclude that } f(X) = \log \det(P^T X^{-1} P).\]
\[\text{Hypothesis: you may use } \begin{bmatrix} A & B \\ B^T & C \end{bmatrix}^{-1} = \begin{bmatrix} 0 & 0 \\ 0 & C^{-1} \end{bmatrix} + \begin{bmatrix} -I \\ C^{-1} B^T \end{bmatrix} (A - B C^{-1} B^T)^{-1} \begin{bmatrix} -I \\ C^{-1} B^T \end{bmatrix}^T,\ \text{if } C \text{ and } A - B C^{-1} B^T \text{ are invertible}.\]

\[\text{Definition: } X \text{ is a random variable on } \mathbf{R}^n \text{ with density } p \text{ with respect to Lebesgue measure.}\]
\[\text{Hypothesis: } p \text{ is log-concave on } \mathbf{R}^n.\]
\[\text{Definition: } g : \mathbf{R}^n \to \mathbf{R}.\]
\[\text{Definition: } Y = g(X).\]
\[\text{Hypothesis: } g \text{ is affine and not constant.}\]
\[\text{Goal: determine whether } Y \text{ has a log-concave density with respect to Lebesgue measure on } \mathbf{R}.\]
\[\text{If true, prove it; if false, give a counterexample.}\]

\[\text{Definition: } X \text{ is an } \mathbf{R}^n\text{-valued random variable with density } p : \mathbf{R}^n \to \mathbf{R}_+.\]\[\text{Hypothesis: } p \text{ is log-concave on } \mathbf{R}^n.\]\[\text{Definition: } g : \mathbf{R}^n \to \mathbf{R}.\]\[\text{Definition: } Y = g(X).\]\[\text{Hypothesis: } g \text{ is convex.}\]\[\text{Definition: } F(a) = \mathbf{prob}(Y \le a) \text{ for } a \in \mathbf{R}.\]\[\text{Goal: determine whether } F \text{ is log-concave in } a \in \mathbf{R}; \text{ either prove it or give a counterexample.}\]

\[\text{Definition: } X \text{ is a random variable on } \mathbf{R}^n \text{ with a log-concave probability density with respect to Lebesgue measure.}\]
\[\text{Definition: } g : \mathbf{R}^n \to \mathbf{R} \text{ is measurable, and } Y = g(X).\]
\[\text{Definition: } (s)_+ = \max\{s,0\}.\]
\[\text{Definition: for } a \in \mathbf{R},\ T(a) = \mathbf{E}\big((Y-a)_+\big).\]
\[\text{Hypothesis: } g \text{ is concave.}\]
\[\text{Hypothesis: the expectation } \mathbf{E}\big((Y-a)_+\big) \text{ exists for all relevant } a.\]
\[\text{Goal: determine whether } T(a) \text{ is a convex function of } a \in \mathbf{R} \text{ and whether it is log-concave in } a \in \mathbf{R}; \text{ prove the true statements or give a counterexample to any false statement.}\]

\[\text{Definition: } a \in \mathbb{R}^n.\]
\[\text{Definition: } C = \{(a_{\pi_1}, a_{\pi_2}, \ldots, a_{\pi_n}) \mid (\pi_1, \pi_2, \ldots, \pi_n) \text{ is a permutation of } (1,2,\ldots,n)\} \subseteq \mathbb{R}^n.\]
\[\text{Definition: For } y \in \mathbb{R}^n,\ S_C(y) = \max_{x \in C} y^T x.\]
\[\text{Definition: For any } u \in \mathbb{R}^n,\ u_{[1]}, u_{[2]}, \ldots, u_{[n]} \text{ are the components of } u \text{ arranged in nonincreasing order.}\]
\[\text{Goal: For every } y \in \mathbb{R}^n,\ prove that } S_C(y) = a_{[1]}y_{[1]} + a_{[2]}y_{[2]} + \cdots + a_{[n]}y_{[n]}.\]

Definition: $a \in \mathbb{R}^n$.\\
Definition: $C \subset \mathbb{R}^n$ is the set of all permutations of $a$, i.e.,
$$
C = \{(a_{\pi_1}, a_{\pi_2}, \ldots, a_{\pi_n}) \mid (\pi_1, \pi_2, \ldots, \pi_n) \text{ is a permutation of } (1,2,\ldots,n)\}.
$$\\
Definition: For $y \in \mathbb{R}^n$, define the support function
$$
S_C(y) = \sup\{y^T z \mid z \in C\}.
$$\\
Definition: For $u \in \mathbb{R}^n$, let $u_{[1]} \ge u_{[2]} \ge \cdots \ge u_{[n]}$ be the components of $u$ sorted in nonincreasing order.\\
Definition: For $k=1,\ldots,n$, define
$$
s_k(u) = u_{[1]} + u_{[2]} + \cdots + u_{[k]}.
$$\\
Definition: $x \in \mathbb{R}^n$.\\
Goal: prove that
$$
x^T y \le S_C(y) \quad \text{for all } y \in \mathbb{R}^n
$$
holds if and only if
$$
s_k(x) \le s_k(a), \quad k = 1,\ldots,n-1, \qquad s_n(x) = s_n(a).
$$

\[\text{Definition: } a \in \mathbb{R}^n.\]
\[\text{Definition: } C \subset \mathbb{R}^n \text{ is the set of all permutations of } a, \text{ i.e., } C = \{(a_{\pi_1}, a_{\pi_2}, \ldots, a_{\pi_n}) \mid (\pi_1, \pi_2, \ldots, \pi_n) \text{ is a permutation of } (1,2,\ldots,n)\}.\]
\[\text{Definition: } S_C(y) = \sup_{z \in C} y^T z.\]
\[\text{Definition: } S_C^*(x) = \sup_{y \in \mathbb{R}^n} (x^T y - S_C(y)).\]
\[\text{Definition: } x \in \mathbb{R}^n \text{ is majorized by } a \text{ if, after rearranging both vectors in nonincreasing order, } \sum_{i=1}^k x_{[i]} \le \sum_{i=1}^k a_{[i]} \text{ for } k=1,\ldots,n-1, \text{ and } \sum_{i=1}^n x_i = \sum_{i=1}^n a_i.\]
\[\text{Goal: prove that } S_C^*(x) = \begin{cases} 0 & \text{if } x \text{ is majorized by } a,\\ +\infty & \text{otherwise.} \end{cases}\]
\[\text{Goal: deduce that } x \text{ is a convex combination of the permutations of } a \text{ if and only if } a \text{ majorizes } x.\]

Definition: $f : \mathbf{R}_{++}^2 \to \mathbf{R}$ is given by $f(x)=x_1^{\theta_1}x_2^{\theta_2}$.\\
Definition: $x \in \mathbf{R}_{++}^2$.\\
Definition: $\theta \in \mathbf{R}^2$.\\
Hypothesis: $\theta \succeq 0$, i.e., $\theta_1 \ge 0$ and $\theta_2 \ge 0$.\\
Hypothesis: $1^T\theta = 1$.\\
Goal: prove that $f$ is concave on $\mathbf{R}_{++}^2$.

\[\text{Definition: } f : \mathbf{R}_{++}^n \to \mathbf{R}, \quad f(x) = x_1^{\theta_1} \cdots x_n^{\theta_n}.\]
\[\text{Definition: } x = (x_1, \dots, x_n) \in \mathbf{R}_{++}^n, \quad \theta = (\theta_1, \dots, \theta_n) \in \mathbf{R}^n.\]
\[\text{Hypothesis: } \theta \succeq 0, \text{ i.e. } \theta_i \ge 0 \text{ for all } i = 1, \dots, n.\]
\[\text{Hypothesis: } 1^T \theta = 1.\]
\[\text{Goal: prove that } f \text{ is concave on } \mathbf{R}_{++}^n.\]

\[\text{Definition: } n \ge 1,\ \theta = (\theta_1,\ldots,\theta_n) \in \mathbf{R}^n.\]\[\text{Definition: } \mathbf{R}_{++}^n = \{x \in \mathbf{R}^n \mid x_i > 0,\ i=1,\ldots,n\}.\]\[\text{Definition: } f : \mathbf{R}_{++}^n \to \mathbf{R},\quad f(x) = x_1^{\theta_1} \cdots x_n^{\theta_n}.\]\[\text{Definition: } 1 \in \mathbf{R}^n \text{ is the all-ones vector.}\]\[\text{Hypothesis: } \theta \succeq 0,\ \text{i.e. } \theta_i \ge 0 \text{ for all } i.\]\[\text{Hypothesis: } 1^T \theta \le 1.\]\[\text{Goal: prove that } f \text{ is concave on } \mathbf{R}_{++}^n.\]

\[\text{Definition: } n \in \mathbf{N}.\]
\[\text{Definition: } \theta = (\theta_1,\dots,\theta_n) \in \mathbf{R}^n.\]
\[\text{Definition: } \mathbf{R}_{++}^n = \{x \in \mathbf{R}^n \mid x_i > 0,\ i=1,\dots,n\}.\]
\[\text{Definition: } f : \mathbf{R}_{++}^n \to \mathbf{R}, \quad f(x) = x_1^{\theta_1} \cdots x_n^{\theta_n}.\]
\[\text{Hypothesis: } \theta \preceq 0, \text{ i.e. } \theta_i \le 0 \text{ for all } i=1,\dots,n.\]
\[\text{Goal: prove that } f \text{ is convex on } \mathbf{R}_{++}^n.\]

\[\begin{aligned}
&\text{Definition: } n \ge 1.\\
&\text{Definition: } \theta=(\theta_1,\ldots,\theta_n) \in \mathbf{R}^n.\\
&\text{Definition: } \mathbf{R}_{++}^n = \{x \in \mathbf{R}^n \mid x_i > 0 \text{ for all } i\}.\\
&\text{Definition: } f: \mathbf{R}_{++}^n \to \mathbf{R}, \quad f(x)=x_1^{\theta_1}\cdots x_n^{\theta_n}.\\
&\text{Hypothesis: } 1^T\theta = 1.\\
&\text{Hypothesis: exactly one component of } \theta \text{ is positive.}\\
&\text{Hypothesis: equivalently, one component of } \theta \text{ is } >0 \text{ and all other components are } \le 0.\\
&\text{Goal: prove that } f \text{ is convex on } \mathbf{R}_{++}^n.
\end{aligned}\]

Definition: $n \in \mathbf{N}$.\\
Definition: $\theta = (\theta_1,\dots,\theta_n) \in \mathbf{R}^n$.\\
Definition: $\mathbf{R}_{++}^n = \{x \in \mathbf{R}^n \mid x_i > 0,\ i=1,\dots,n\}$.\\
Definition: $f : \mathbf{R}_{++}^n \to \mathbf{R}$ is given by $f(x) = x_1^{\theta_1} \cdots x_n^{\theta_n}$.\\
Hypothesis: $1^T \theta \ge 1$.\\
Hypothesis: exactly one component of $\theta$ is positive; that is, there exists a unique index $i$ such that $\theta_i > 0$, and $\theta_j \le 0$ for all $j \ne i$.\\
Goal: prove that $f$ is convex on $\mathbf{R}_{++}^n$.

\[\text{Definition: } f,g : \mathbf{R}^n \to \mathbf{R},\quad f(x)=\|x\|_1,\quad g(x)=\frac{1}{2}\|x\|_2^2.\]\[\text{Definition: } h : \mathbf{R}^n \to \mathbf{R},\quad h(x)=\inf_{y\in\mathbf{R}^n} \bigl(f(y)+g(x-y)\bigr)=\inf_{y\in\mathbf{R}^n} \left(\|y\|_1+\frac{1}{2}\|x-y\|_2^2\right).\]\[\text{Definition: For } u\in\mathbf{R},\ \phi(u)=\begin{cases} u^2/2, & |u|\le 1,\\ |u|-1/2, & |u|>1. \end{cases}\]\[\text{Definition: } x=(x_1,\dots,x_n)\in\mathbf{R}^n.\]\[\text{Goal: Prove that } h(x)=\sum_{i=1}^n \phi(x_i) \text{ for all } x\in\mathbf{R}^n.\]

\[	ext{Definition: } n \geq 1.\]
\[	ext{Definition: } h : \mathbf{R} 	o \mathbf{R}.\]
\[	ext{Hypothesis: } h 	ext{ is convex on } \mathbf{R}.\]
\[	ext{Hypothesis: } h 	ext{ is nondecreasing on } \mathbf{R}.\]
\[	ext{Hypothesis: } \operatorname{dom} h = \mathbf{R}.\]
\[	ext{Hypothesis: } h(t) = h(0) 	ext{ for all } t \leq 0.\]
\[	ext{Definition: } f : \mathbf{R}^n 	o \mathbf{R}, \quad f(x) = h(\|x\|_2).\]
\[	ext{Goal: prove that } f 	ext{ is convex on } \mathbf{R}^n.\]

Definition: $h : \mathbf{R} \to \mathbf{R}$.\\
Hypothesis: $h$ is convex.\\
Hypothesis: $h$ is nondecreasing.\\
Hypothesis: $\operatorname{dom} h = \mathbf{R}$.\\
Hypothesis: for all $t \le 0$, $h(t) = h(0)$.\\
Definition: $f : \mathbf{R}^n \to \mathbf{R}$ is defined by $f(x) = h(\|x\|_2)$.\\
Definition: $y \in \mathbf{R}^n$.\\
Goal: prove that $f^*(y) = h^*(\|y\|_2)$.

\[\text{Definition: } p > 1,\ x \in \mathbf{R}^n,\ \text{and } f(x) = \frac{1}{p}\|x\|_2^p.\]
\[\text{Definition: } h : \mathbf{R} \to \mathbf{R},\quad h(t) = \frac{1}{p}\max\{0,t\}^p = \begin{cases} \frac{1}{p} t^p & t \ge 0,\\ 0 & t < 0. \end{cases}\]
\[\text{Hypothesis: If } g : \mathbf{R}^n \to \mathbf{R} \text{ is defined by } g(x) = h(\|x\|_2),\ \text{then } g^*(y) = h^*(\|y\|_2) \text{ for all } y \in \mathbf{R}^n.\]
\[\text{Goal: use this result to derive the convex conjugate of } f.\]
